title: Decagon and OpenAI deliver high-performance, fully automated customer support at scale
summary: OpenAI describes Decagon using its models to automate customer support conversations at scale without human intervention.
note: Direct support-automation case study should attach to customer service.

title: Zendesk unveils AI-powered CX platform with sophisticated agents and intelligent copilots
summary: The report describes AI agents and copilots automating more customer support interactions and case resolution.
note: High-quality AI media piece tied directly to customer support workflows.

title: Put AI to work: Automate and Scale Financial Operations
summary: OpenAI highlights automating reconciliation, closing work, and finance back-office processes with AI.
note: Finance operations automation is a direct bookkeeping and back-office signal.

title: Docyt AI Unveils GARY, the World's First AI Bookkeeper
summary: Docyt says its AI bookkeeper automates manual bookkeeping and continuous reconciliation, dramatically shortening the monthly close.
note: The article is explicitly about AI bookkeepers replacing manual bookkeeping work.

title: Basis scales accounting by turning OpenAI model progress into trusted agents
summary: OpenAI says Basis uses AI agents to automate reconciliations, journal entries, and financial summaries inside accounting firms.
note: Accounting-firm workflow automation should map to bookkeeping rather than stay broad.

title: How Numeric is automating accounting workflows with OpenAI
summary: Numeric uses OpenAI models to automate accounting close work, reconciliation, and finance review workflows.
note: Another direct accounting automation case with clear occupational fit.

title: How Axios uses AI to help deliver high-impact local journalism
summary: Axios describes journalists using AI tools to support reporting and local-news workflows.
note: The article is explicitly framed around journalism work.

title: The Newsroom AI Catalyst: a global program with WAN-IFRA
summary: The program focuses on AI adoption in newsrooms and publisher workflows.
note: This is one of the few newsroom pieces that is specific enough to journalism operations.

title: Particle launches an AI news app to help publishers, instead of just stealing their work
summary: TechCrunch covers an AI news app for consumers and publishers, but the story is mostly about product strategy and media distribution.
note: Publisher/product news is too broad to attach to one occupation with high precision.

title: Measuring AI agent autonomy in practice
summary: Anthropic analyzes millions of agent interactions across many domains, from email triage to cyber operations, without centering one occupation.
note: Cross-domain autonomy research should stay unmatched.

title: One in a million: celebrating the customers shaping AI's future
summary: OpenAI highlights many kinds of customer adoption across writing, coding, research, data analysis, and design.
note: This is a broad customer roundup, not a role-specific workflow signal.

title: The state of enterprise AI
summary: OpenAI summarizes enterprise AI trends across functions and industries rather than one job family.
note: General enterprise trend reporting should not force a role.

title: A business that scales with the value of intelligence
summary: The post argues for the economics of AI across companies but does not describe one occupation's workflow in detail.
note: Broad strategic framing should stay unmatched.

title: OpenAI and journalism
summary: OpenAI outlines partnership and licensing work with publishers and journalism organizations.
note: This is industry-partnership news, not a direct occupation workflow change.

title: Partnership with American Journalism Project to support local news
summary: OpenAI announces funding and API credits for local news organizations to explore AI adoption.
note: Funding and partnership support for an industry should not be treated as one role signal.

title: Introducing OpenAI Academy for News Organizations
summary: OpenAI launches training resources for news organizations to experiment with AI tools.
note: Training and enablement news is still too broad to attach to one occupation.

title: OpenAI and Hearst Content Partnership
summary: OpenAI and Hearst describe a content and product partnership across Hearst brands.
note: Content partnership news is broad and not role-specific enough.

title: Zencoder launches Zen Agents, ushering in a new era of team-based AI for software development
summary: The article describes collaborative coding agents for software teams, but the current role dictionary lacks a clearly equivalent software-developer role.
note: Prefer unmatched over a forced mapping when the dictionary does not fit cleanly.

title: Support Operations Manager | OpenAI
summary: OpenAI seeks a support operations manager to architect automation systems and agentic workflows for customer support at scale.
note: The posting is managerial and cross-functional, so precision-first classification should not downgrade it to a frontline support role.

title: Operations Analyst, User Operations (Trust & Safety) - EMEA | OpenAI
summary: The role focuses on trust, safety, abuse prevention, escalations, and operational risk workflows.
note: Trust and safety operations do not cleanly match the current public role dictionary.

title: User Safety & Risk Operations Analyst | OpenAI
summary: OpenAI describes analysts handling abuse, fraud, compliance, and sensitive inbound risk cases.
note: The safest behavior is unmatched until the dictionary carries a closer trust-and-safety occupation.

title: OpenAI and journalism
summary: The post discusses principles, partnerships, and licensing around journalism rather than automating newsroom tasks directly.
note: Industry policy content should remain unmatched.
